    <item>
      <title>Celtics' Jayson Tatum addresses injury concerns ahead of Game 7</title>
      <description>Given that Tatum is coming off a major injury, it didn't take long before fans sounded the alarms. That's why he wanted to clear the air and ease everyone's minds.</description>

    <item>
      <title>Latest update on Luka Doncic doesn't sound good for Lakers</title>

    <item>
      <title>76ers complete historic series comeback to stun Celtics in Game 7</title>
      <description>The Philadelphia 76ers appeared to be heading toward a first-round playoff exit, facing a 3-1 series deficit to the Boston Celtics. With Saturday's 109-100 win in Game 7, they are heading to the Eastern Conference semifinals for the first time since 2023.</description>
      <pubDate>02 May 2026 22:07:53 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/76ers_complete_historic_series_comeback_to_stun_celtics_in_game_7/s1_13132_43799799</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/76ers_complete_historic_series_comeback_to_stun_celtics_in_game_7/s1_13132_43799799</guid>
      <author>Colby Colwell, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/7/7/778c811558ec3b6870290b35bd795471df10bbad/thumb_16x9/76ers-complete-series-comeback-stun-celtics-game-7.jpg">
        <media:credit> Bill Streicher-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Philadelphia 76ers guard Tyrese Maxey (0) drives past Boston Celtics guard Jaylen Brown (7) during the third quarter at Xfinity Mobile Arena. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Philadelphia 76ers</category>
      <category>Boston Celtics</category>
      <category>New York Knicks</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>Jaylen Brown takes parting shot at Joel Embiid</title>
      <description>Jaylen Brown took his chance to take one final swipe at Joel Embiid with the Boston Celtics’ season coming to an end. The Celtics star spoke to reporters on Saturday after his team lost Game 7 of their first-round series against Embiid’s Philadelphia 76ers. </description>
      <pubDate>03 May 2026 00:11:54 -0400</pubDate>

    <item>
      <title>What Magic can learn from collapse in Game 6 vs. Pistons</title>
      <description>One minute into the second half of Game 6, the Orlando Magic had a 24-point lead and were poised to eliminate the top-seeded Detroit Pistons. They proceeded to score the fewest points ever in an NBA playoff half in a 93-79 loss.</description>
      <pubDate>02 May 2026 12:00:22 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/what_magic_can_learn_from_collapse_in_game_6_vs_pistons/s1_13132_43796385</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/what_magic_can_learn_from_collapse_in_game_6_vs_pistons/s1_13132_43796385</guid>
      <author>Sean Keane, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/d/a/daac474db556785bcfbccc21c4ee5839e884d974/thumb_16x9/magic-learn-collapse-game-6-vs-pistons.jpg">
        <media:credit> Jeremy Reper-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Orlando Magic guard Desmond Bane (3) celebrates with Orlando Magic forward Tristan da Silva (23) in the second quarter against the Detroit Pistons during game six of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Kia Center. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Orlando Magic</category>
      <category>Detroit Pistons</category>

    <item>

    <item>
